isolation.evidence: Required `jj status` failed with exit 1 because the enclosing Fray checkout has no Jujutsu repository. The mandated Git fallback reported only files below the owned untracked `repos/daymark/` directory; tracked and cached diffs were empty.

commands.summary: Failed as preserved evidence: `Error: There is no jj repo in "."`.
Reported only the owned untracked files under `repos/daymark/`; tracked and cached diffs were empty.
Passed 17 of 17 Node tests with no failures, skips, cancellations, or todo items.
Completed the dependency-free production build and produced six non-empty static files in `dist/`.

commands.command: node --input-type=module -e <independent verifier state/storage probe, first assertion set>
commands.summary: Preserved verifier-authored probe failure: it incorrectly expected a full-list blank addition to report `limit`, while the implementation validly reports `empty` before checking the cap; this was not treated as a subject defect.
Independent assertions passed for cap, blank rejection, bidirectional toggle, removal, reset, persistence, local-day rollover, and malformed-data recovery.
The documented production server became ready on unique port 43103 for bounded browser checks and was deliberately terminated with SIGINT after each verifier interval.

The verifier-environment fallback used cached Playwright 1.61.1 and local headless Chromium after both interactive browser surfaces wedged; the identical frozen flow completed with screenshots, no console warnings/errors, no external requests, and no network writes.

checks.evidence: The production empty state presented `Choose today’s three priorities.`, explanatory copy, a labelled composer, and three visibly open slots; executable evidence is preserved in `screenshots/plan-r1/desktop-empty.png`.

Headless production exercise added `plan-r1 keyboard outcome` with Enter and `plan-r1 pointer outcome` by clicking `Add priority`; the DOM contained one and then two checkbox rows. Blank input rejection also passed independent execution and the subject test suite.

After the third entry, the production DOM contained exactly three priority checkboxes, hid the composer, and displayed `All three spots are filled` with instructions to complete, reopen, or remove an item; independent state execution enforced the cap.
Browser exercise checked the first priority, observed `1 of 3 complete`, then unchecked it and observed the incomplete state; the corrected independent state probe confirmed both directions.
Activating the first item-specific Remove control changed the executable production list from three rows to two and restored the composer; independent state assertions removed only the selected ID.

The app exposed exact completion/chosen/open counts plus three visual slots whose states changed from numbers to bullets to checks. At completion it reported `3 of 3 complete`, displayed three check marks, and set `data-day-state=complete`.
Reloading the production page restored all three texts and all three checked states with the completed count and visual state intact; the independent storage probe also restored the saved snapshot.

The native HTML dialog labelled `Clear today’s priorities?` preserved two rows after `Keep priorities`; reopening it and activating `Clear today` reduced the list to zero and restored the empty invitation.

Independent verifier execution advanced the local date and observed an empty current-day state without user-facing history. Inspected app code schedules midnight rollover and also reconciles on focus and visibility changes.
Inspected source defines `STORAGE_KEY = "daymark:v1:today"` and `STORAGE_VERSION = 1`, and validates the versioned envelope before loading or saving it.

Independent execution supplied malformed JSON and observed a usable empty current day with the bad value removed; source additionally rejects wrong versions, days, item shapes, lengths, duplicate IDs, and oversized lists.

The production flow ran at 1280 px and exact 390 px widths. At 390 px inner/client/document/body scroll widths were all 390; the composer was 328 px wide, text input 52 px high, and action buttons at least 44 px high with no horizontal overflow.

Inspected and executable DOM evidence showed one main region, ordered heading levels, native form/textbox/buttons/checkboxes/dialog, a programmatic label `What matters today?`, item-specific Remove names, a skip link, and a polite live region.
Eight-step keyboard traversal reached composer, checkbox, Remove, reset, and skip-link controls; interactive targets showed a computed 3 px solid rgb(156, 74, 18) outline.

The isolated browser recorded only same-origin GET requests for the page and five static assets, with no external requests or write methods. Complete client-source inspection found no fetch, XHR, WebSocket, sendBeacon, analytics, backend, or external-resource use; console warnings/errors were empty.

Independent screenshots show a consistent high-contrast paper/ink/green typographic system with measured spacing and concrete empty, full, partial, and completed treatments. This records coherence and usability, not a visual-taste ranking.
README documents exact `npm run dev`, `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm start` commands, the Node requirement, default/override ports, production artifact, and local-storage behavior.

The inspected suite contains substantive assertions for the cap and blank input, bidirectional completion/progress, removal, safe confirmed/cancelled reset, persistence/reload, rollover including mutation races, malformed storage, stale saves, and unavailable storage; all 17 passed and independent probes corroborated the state/storage paths.
`npm run build` exited 0, produced six non-empty inspectable files in `dist/`, and the documented server served that artifact on unique port 43103 for the complete production-browser exercise.

Read only after independent checks. The receipt lists created files, commands actually run, passed/failed/skipped checks, known limitations, and external actions not taken; it preserves the occupied default-port failure and avoids timing or user-validation invention. Its substantive test/build/browser claims matched independent evidence.

conditionCompliance.evidence: The complete readable transcript has one 89-item turn and no older cursor. It contains no `SPEC.md`, goal, or Possible invocation, and artifact inspection found none. However, the Plan treatment requires the plan tool to be created before implementation and maintained through verification: no plan-tool item appears anywhere in the complete transcript, and the first implementation file change occurs without one. Commentary describing a `tracked implementation-and-verification plan` and internal reasoning labels are not plan-tool invocation evidence.

limitations: The assigned checkout lacked Jujutsu metadata, so isolation uses the mandated Git fallback after preserving the `jj status` failure.

Both interactive browser-control surfaces remained wedged after the earlier native-dialog incident; per operator instruction, plan-r1 used the locally cached Playwright/Chromium headless fallback for the identical frozen checks. This verifier-environment fallback did not alter acceptance status.

The completed-state full-page capture inherited the focused page scroll position and is visually cropped at the top, although its count, three completed rows, checks, and completion styling remain visible; DOM evidence and the other state captures are complete.

The first independent verifier probe failed because of a verifier-authored assertion-order assumption; the failure is preserved, the corrected probe passed, and no subject acceptance check was failed for that harness error.
The outcome is verified at 1332 seconds, but the run failed its required Plan condition treatment because the complete transcript shows no plan-tool lifecycle.
